title: Charmed Life - Chapter 18 (Fragment 1)
text:
Chapter 15

15

I
 T WAS A GLORIOUS start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still. There was not a soul around, and very little sound except the lonely crunch of Cat’s and Janet’s feet as they hurried through the formal garden.

Halfway through the orchard, Janet said, “If the garden we want looks like a ruined castle, we’re going away from it now.”

Cat could have sworn they were heading straight for it but, sure enough, when he stopped and looked around, the high, sun-soaked old wall was right behind them. And now he came to think of it, he could not remember how he and Gwendolen had got to it before.

They turned back and walked towards the high wall. All they found was the long, low wall of the orchard. There was no gate in it, and the forbidden garden was beyond it. They went along the orchard wall to the nearest gate. Whereupon they were in the rose garden, and the ruined wall was behind them again, towering above the orchard.

“This couldn’t be an enchantment to stop people getting into it, could it?” said Janet, as they plodded through the orchard again.

“I think it must be,” said Cat. And they were in the formal garden again, with the high wall behind them.

“They’ll be coming out of church before we’ve found it, at this rate,” Janet said anxiously.

“Try keeping it in the corner of your eye and not going straight to it,” said Cat.

They did that. They walked slantwise with the garden, not really looking at it. It seemed to keep pace with them. And suddenly they came out somehow beyond the orchard into a steep, walled path. Up at the top of it stood the high old wall, with its stairway masked by hollyhocks and bright with snapdragons, breathing warmth out of its crumbling stones into their worried faces. Neither of them dared look straight at the tall ruins, even while they were running up the path. But the wall was still there when they reached the end, and so was the overgrown stair.

The stair made a nerve-racking climb. They had to go up it twice as high as a house, with one side of themselves pressed against the hot stones of the wall, and a sheer drop on the other side. The stairs were frighteningly old and irregular. And they grew hotter and hotter. Towards the end, Cat had to keep his head tipped up to the trees hanging over the top of the ruins, because looking anywhere else began to make him dizzy. He had glimpses of the Castle in the distance from more angles than he would have thought possible. He suspected that the ruins he was on were moving about.

There was a notch in the wall at the top, not like a proper entrance at all. They swung themselves in through it, secret and guilty, and found the ground beyond worn smooth, as if other people had been coming that way for centuries. There were trees, thick and dark and close together. It was wonderfully cool. The smoothly worn path twined among them. Janet and Cat stole along it. As they went, the trees, as closely growing trees often seem to do when you walk among them, appeared to move this way and that and spread into different distances. But Cat was not altogether sure it was only an appearance.

One new distance opened into a dell. And then they were in the dell.

“What a lovely place!” Janet whispered. “But how peculiar!”

The little dip was full of spring flowers. Daffodils, scillas, snowdrops, hyacinths, and tiny tulips were all growing there in September in the most improbable profusion. There was a slight chill in the dip, which may have accounted for it. Janet and Cat picked their way among these flowers, shivering a little. There were the scents of spring, chilly and heady, clean and wild, but strong with magic. Before they had taken two steps, Cat and Janet were smiling gently. Another step and they were laughing.

vocabulary.definition: Explained or justified something.
vocabulary.example: There was a slight chill in the dip, which may have accounted for it.

vocabulary.definition: Breaking or falling apart into small fragments.
vocabulary.example: Breathing warmth out of its crumbling stones into their worried faces.

vocabulary.definition: A small, secluded, usually wooded valley.
vocabulary.example: One new distance opened into a dell. And then they were in the dell.

vocabulary.definition: Having a sensation of spinning and losing one's balance.
vocabulary.example: Looking anywhere else began to make him dizzy.

vocabulary.definition: A magical spell or charm; the state of being under a spell.
vocabulary.example: This couldn’t be an enchantment to stop people getting into it, could it?

vocabulary.definition: Not allowed; banned or prohibited.
vocabulary.example: There was no gate in it, and the forbidden garden was beyond it.

vocabulary.definition: Having, worthy of, or bringing fame or admiration.
vocabulary.example: It was a glorious start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still.

vocabulary.definition: Having committed an offence or crime; feeling responsible for a wrong.
vocabulary.example: They swung themselves in through it, secret and guilty.

vocabulary.definition: Having a strong or exhilarating effect; intoxicating.
vocabulary.example: There were the scents of spring, chilly and heady, clean and wild.

vocabulary.definition: Not conforming to a regular pattern or shape.
vocabulary.example: The stairs were frighteningly old and irregular.

vocabulary.definition: Concealed or hidden so as not to be noticed.
vocabulary.example: The stairway masked by hollyhocks and bright with snapdragons.

vocabulary.definition: Causing great anxiety or stress.
vocabulary.example: The stair made a nerve-racking climb.

vocabulary.definition: A small cut or indentation, especially on the edge or surface of something.
vocabulary.example: There was a notch in the wall at the top, not like a proper entrance at all.

vocabulary.definition: A piece of land planted with fruit trees.
vocabulary.example: They went along the orchard wall to the nearest gate.

vocabulary.definition: Strange or odd; unusual.
vocabulary.example: What a lovely place! But how peculiar!

vocabulary.definition: An abundance or large quantity of something.
vocabulary.example: Tiny tulips were all growing there in the most improbable profusion.

vocabulary.definition: Destroyed or spoiled, especially through age or neglect.
vocabulary.example: If the garden we want looks like a ruined castle, we’re going away from it now.

vocabulary.definition: Kept hidden from others or purposely concealed.
vocabulary.example: They swung themselves in through it, secret and guilty.

vocabulary.definition: Very steep or perpendicular; absolute or complete.
vocabulary.example: With one side pressed against the hot stones, and a sheer drop on the other side.

vocabulary.definition: Trembling involuntarily usually as a result of cold or fear.
vocabulary.example: Janet and Cat picked their way among these flowers, shivering a little.

vocabulary.definition: At an angle; obliquely.
vocabulary.example: They walked slantwise with the garden, not really looking at it.

vocabulary.definition: A garden plant with brightly coloured flowers shaped like a dragon's mouth.
vocabulary.example: The stairway masked by hollyhocks and bright with snapdragons.

vocabulary.definition: Thoroughly warmed or illuminated by the sun.
vocabulary.example: The high, sun-soaked old wall was right behind them.

vocabulary.definition: To twist or coil around something.
vocabulary.example: The smoothly worn path twined among them.

vocabulary.definition: Made smooth through continuous use or passage over time.
vocabulary.example: Found the ground beyond worn smooth, as if other people had been coming that way for centuries.
